MILĀ PULSE · WEEKLY POINTS
This
Week
Jul 13 – Jul 19, 2026 · Issue 3F
Focus: Guest Satisfaction & Reviews
01FOCUS
✎ Editorial · manager-written
This week our main focus is guest satisfaction and reviews. Be intentional about creating memorable experiences, strengthen every guest interaction, and confidently offer review cards to guests who are having a great time. Stay present, read your tables, and elevate the experience from the first greeting to the final goodbye.
4.8
★★★★★
20,319 Google reviews
Tonight’s goal: deliver excellence so consistently that guests feel compelled to leave a review. Take control of the table, anticipate needs, and end with a strong farewell. Reviews are earned through details.
02CORE VALUE
“
Integrity is doing the right thing, even when no one is watching.
03 · 04KNOWLEDGE & SOP
SERVICE KNOWLEDGE
Taking Orders & Suggestive Selling

Guide guests through the menu while subtly upselling. Use storytelling to enhance dish expectations. Upsell naturally through enthusiasm and expertise — keeping dietary restrictions and preferences at the forefront of your mind.

GENERAL KNOWLEDGE
Timekeeping Policy

Clock in and out for all shifts and meal periods using Toast. Clock in no more than five minutes before your scheduled shift and clock out promptly after completing your work. All hours worked must be recorded — working off the clock is not permitted; schedule changes or additional hours need manager approval in advance.

Shift swap & pick-up: submit and get approval no later than the day before the shift. In an emergency, contact the managers scheduled that day through the email channels. Last-minute schedule changes cannot be accommodated.

ZERO TOLERANCE
Professional Conduct

Serious insubordination, abusive language, deliberate policy violations, or conduct that damages the safety, integrity, or reputation of the workplace may result in disciplinary action, up to and including termination.

✎ Manager poster · tap to zoom
SERVICE SOP
Teamwork & Cross-Department Support

Every team member plays an important role in delivering a seamless guest experience. Whether running food and beverages, seating guests, bussing, or resetting tables, always be ready to support one another across departments. Working together with urgency, respect, and a positive attitude strengthens the entire restaurant.

THE FIVE PILLARS
RespectTreat everyone with kindness
SupportHelp each other succeed
UrgencyMove with purpose and stay present
PositivityBring good energy every day
TeamworkOne team, one goal
05BAR
✎ Manager poster · tap to zoom
SPIRIT KNOWLEDGE
Bourbon & Rye

Bold, rich, iconically American — crafted from grain, shaped by time, defined by tradition. Bourbon is smooth, sweet and full-bodied; rye is spicy, bold and complex. Two styles, one heritage.

OUR SELECTION · WITH TASTING NOTES
Basil HaydenLight-bodied and smooth — vanilla, honey, toasted oak, soft spice, clean finish
Michter’s Single BarrelRich and balanced — caramel, dried fruit, oak with a hint of smoke and spice
Maker’s MarkWheat-forward bourbon; smooth and mellow — vanilla, caramel
Old St. NickClassic, full-bodied — oak, vanilla, baking spice
Basil Hayden Dark RyeMellow rye; smooth and approachable — caramel, dried fruit, oak
Angel’s Envy RyeFinished in Caribbean rum casks — spicy, tropical, smooth
THE LAW & THE CLASSICS

Bourbon: made in the USA · ≥51% corn · aged in new charred oak · barrel entry ≤125 proof · bottled ≥80 proof. Rye: ≥51% rye in the mash bill. House distillery focus: Buffalo Trace — Frankfort, Kentucky, one of America’s oldest continuously operating distilleries.

Classic cocktails: Old Fashioned · Manhattan · Vieux Carré · Mint Julep.

06WINE
✎ Manager poster · tap to zoom
✓ Pulled from your verified menu
🍯Château Suduiraut Sauternes ’13 · $40 / 3ozBordeaux · Sémillon 90 / SB 10 · apricot, honey, white peach · lush & balanced$403 oz
👑Château d’Yquem Sauternes ’16 · $60 / 1ozThe benchmark · Sémillon 80 / SB 20 · pineapple, mango, honeycomb, vanilla, saffron$601 oz
🍇Florio Marsala Semisecco Riserva ’15 · $20 / 3ozSicily · Grillo, Inzolia, Catarratto · dried fig, caramel, toasted almond · “solera” aged$203 oz
🥃Taylor Fladgate 20 Yr Tawny Porto NV · $25 / 3ozDouro · traditional blend · dried fig, prune, caramel, hazelnut, mocha · nutty nightcap$253 oz
SERVICE TIPS

Serve Sauternes & Marsala slightly chilled (45–50°F) · Porto slightly cooler than room temperature (55–60°F) · all pours are 3 oz, designed for savoring (d’Yquem 1 oz) · these wines offer depth, balance and a memorable end to any dining experience — pour with intention, present with pride.

08ALLERGEN OF THE WEEK

SHELLFISH
Allergy to crustaceans and/or mollusks.
Shrimp · scallops · mussels · lobster
